---
title: "example"
author: "Xukun Zhu"
date: "2026-04-08"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(rstan)
options(mc.cores = parallel::detectCores())
rstan_options(auto_write = TRUE)
```

# 1.Data Simulation
```{r}
set.seed(2026)
N <- 500      
J <- 5        
P_tv <- 2      
P_fixed <- 2   

# Generate Fixed Covariates (N x P_fixed)
X_fixed <- matrix(rnorm(N * P_fixed), nrow = N, ncol = P_fixed)

# Generate Time-Varying Covariates for each transition (N x P_tv)
# Simulating slightly varying values across stages for the same patient
X_tv_stage1 <- matrix(rnorm(N * P_tv), nrow = N)
X_tv_stage2 <- X_tv_stage1 + matrix(rnorm(N * P_tv, 0, 0.1), nrow = N)
X_tv_stage3 <- X_tv_stage2 + matrix(rnorm(N * P_tv, 0, 0.1), nrow = N)
X_tv_stage4 <- X_tv_stage3 + matrix(rnorm(N * P_tv, 0, 0.1), nrow = N)

# Define the Mask Matrix for time-varying covariates (P_tv x 4)
# Let's simulate structural missingness: 
# Covariate 1 (e.g., BMI) is measured at all 4 transitions (1, 1, 1, 1)
# Covariate 2 (e.g., MELD) is structurally missing at transition 1 (0, 1, 1, 1)
mask_tv <- matrix(c(1, 1, 1, 1,
                    0, 1, 1, 1), nrow = P_tv, byrow = TRUE)

# Simulate True Parameters
true_beta_fixed <- c(0.5, -0.3)
true_beta_tv    <- c(-0.2, 0.8)

# Generate the Categorical Outcome Y based on the sequential logit probabilities
Y <- numeric(N)
for (i in 1:N) {
  logit_p <- numeric(J - 1)
  
  # Calculate linear predictors (incorporating the mask mechanism)
  logit_p[1] <- sum(X_tv_stage1[i, ] * (true_beta_tv * mask_tv[, 1])) + sum(X_fixed[i, ] * true_beta_fixed)
  logit_p[2] <- sum(X_tv_stage2[i, ] * (true_beta_tv * mask_tv[, 2])) + sum(X_fixed[i, ] * true_beta_fixed)
  logit_p[3] <- sum(X_tv_stage3[i, ] * (true_beta_tv * mask_tv[, 3])) + sum(X_fixed[i, ] * true_beta_fixed)
  logit_p[4] <- sum(X_tv_stage4[i, ] * (true_beta_tv * mask_tv[, 4])) + sum(X_fixed[i, ] * true_beta_fixed)
  
  # Convert to conditional probabilities (inv_logit)
  cond_prob <- plogis(logit_p)
  
  # Convert conditional probabilities to categorical probabilities (pi)
  pi <- numeric(J)
  prod_term <- 1.0
  for (j in 1:(J - 1)) {
    pi[j] <- prod_term * (1 - cond_prob[j])
    prod_term <- prod_term * cond_prob[j]
  }
  pi[J] <- prod_term
  
  # Sample the final stage Y[i] based on pi
  Y[i] <- sample(1:J, size = 1, prob = pi)
}
```

# 2. Model Fitting
```{r}
stan_data <- list(
  N = N,
  P_tv = P_tv,
  P_fixed = P_fixed,
  J = J,
  X_tv_stage1 = X_tv_stage1,
  X_tv_stage2 = X_tv_stage2,
  X_tv_stage3 = X_tv_stage3,
  X_tv_stage4 = X_tv_stage4,
  X_fixed = X_fixed,
  mask_tv = mask_tv,
  Y = Y
)

compiled_model <- stan_model(file = 'Bayesian Sequential Logistic Regression.stan')

fit <- sampling(
  object = compiled_model,  
  data = stan_data,
  iter = 2000,            
  warmup = 1000,
  chains = 4
)
```

```{r}
print(fit, pars = c("beta_fixed_global", "beta_tv_global"))
```

```{r}
traceplot(fit, pars = c("beta_fixed_global"))
```
